text.title: Interview structure, data collection, and post-interview processing
text.summary: Four-phase interview structure (Opening/Exploration/Digging/Closing), data categories to collect, and post-interview processing to extract signal patterns across multiple interviews.

rule.statement: Open with a neutral topic (their work context), explore specific past behavior, dig into cost and workarounds, then close by asking for referrals. Do not mention your idea until after the exploration phase — if at all.
rule.rationale: Opening with your idea frames all subsequent questions as evaluation of your solution. Opening with their context produces uncontaminated behavioral data.

list.item: Opening (2 min): "I'm trying to understand how people deal with [GENERAL AREA]. Tell me about your experience with [TOPIC]."
Exploration (15 min): "Walk me through the last time you [DID THING]", "What was hardest about that?", "How did you solve it?", "What else have you tried?"
Digging (5 min): "Why is that important?", "Can you give me a specific example?", "How much time/money does this cost you?"
Closing (3 min): "Who else should I talk to about this?", "What should I have asked that I didn't?"

rule.statement: Collect five categories of signal in every interview: Problem (does it exist, frequency, severity), Current solution (what they already use and why), Willingness to pay (have they paid for solutions before), Decision process (who decides, timeline, criteria), Urgency (active vs passive searching).
rule.rationale: Missing any category leaves gaps in the problem hypothesis. Decision process is the most commonly skipped and most important for B2B sales.

rule.statement: Immediately after the interview, record exact quotes (not paraphrases). Rate each signal as Strong / Weak / No signal. After 5+ interviews, run a cross-transcript synthesis pass looking for repeated problems (3+ independent mentions).
rule.rationale: Paraphrasing introduces analyst bias into the data. Exact quotes preserve the specific words and dollar amounts that are the highest-value signals. Pattern recognition requires 3+ independent mentions to be credible.

rule.statement: Never let an agent score "problem severity" on a 1-10 scale from text alone. Require minimum quote count per pattern (3+ independent mentions from separate interviewees) before treating a pattern as validated.
rule.rationale: Agents produce confident-sounding severity scores from thin data. A severity of "8/10" from one interview is not a validated problem.

text.reference: Keyword-based signal classifier for post-interview notes
Prompt for generating compliant interview questions
